prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye be askin' why the firmament above be painted such a brilliant azure? Listen well, ye landlubber! 'Tis the very sunlight dancin' and bouncin' through the vast ocean of air, like me ship ridin' waves! Them learned men speak of somethin' called "scatterin'" - where the bright rays o' the sun get tossed about by tiny bits floatin' in the sky, like seabirds flappin' their wings. The blue waves of light, they be shorter an' nimbler than other colors, so they leap and bound more freely through the heavens. Red and yellow, they be slower, heavy as an anchor, an' don't scatter near as much. So when ye look up, ye be seein' the blue light scattered every which way, coverin' the sky like the deepest part o' the Caribbean! By Davy Jones' locker, 'tis a marvel of nature that would make even the saltiest sea dog pause and marvel! Now pass the rum, ye swab, for all this learned talkin' be makin' me throat dry! *takes a hearty swig*
